# UC-19.1.37 · Proxmox vzdump Scheduled Backup Task Failure Detection

## Description

Node-local vzdump jobs back up guests to file or Proxmox Backup Server targets. A failed vzdump row in the Proxmox task log means the guest was not protected on that schedule, which is often noticed only after a ransomware or corruption event.

## Value

Restores confidence in RPO for Proxmox guests by surfacing failed vzdump tasks immediately instead of waiting for the next DR test.

## Implementation

Install the Proxmox TA or forward parsed task logs from each PVE node. Normalize `task_type` to vzdump for backup jobs. Extract `status`, `vmid`, `upid`, and error text. Alert on any non-OK terminal status. Optionally join to a CMDB of guests that require daily backups.

## SPL

```spl
index=virtualization sourcetype="proxmox:task" task_type="vzdump" earliest=-24h
| where status!="OK" AND isnotnull(status)
| stats count as failures, values(error_message) as errors by vmid, node, upid
| sort -failures
```

## Visualization

Table (failed vzdump by VM and node), Timeline (backup task outcomes), Single value (failed jobs in last 24h).

## References

- [Proxmox VE Admin Guide — Backup and Restore](https://pve.proxmox.com/pve-docs/pve-admin-guide.html#chapter_backup_restore)
